question: Comment persister les instructions et conventions de projet pour l'agent IA ?

principles.text: Maintenir un fichier d'instructions de projet committed dans le repo (CLAUDE.md pour Claude Code, AGENTS.md pour le standard cross-vendor stewarded par Agentic AI Foundation/Linux Foundation, .cursorrules/.cursor/rules/*.mdc pour Cursor, .clinerules/ pour Cline, .github/copilot-instructions.md pour GitHub Copilot). ATTENTION paradoxe empirique : Gloaguen et al. 2026 (arXiv:2602.11988) montre que les context files peuvent *reduire* la task success rate et augmenter le cout d'inference de 20%+. Recommendation : **keep minimal and non-inferable** — documenter uniquement ce qui ne peut pas etre devine du code source (build/run commands, conventions de nommage custom, constraints techniques non-evidentes). Eviter les listes exhaustives.

sources.name: Gloaguen, Mundler, Muller, Raychev, Vechev 2026 - Evaluating AGENTS.md (arXiv:2602.11988)
sources.quote: context files tend to reduce task success rates compared to providing no repository context, while also increasing inference cost by over 20%

sources.name: Daiki et al. 2025 - On the Use of Agentic Coding Manifests (arXiv:2509.14744, PROFES 2025)
sources.quote: 253 CLAUDE.md files from 242 repositories; Build and Run (77.1%), Implementation Details (71.9%), Architecture (64.8%), Testing (60.5%)

sources.name: Agent READMEs arXiv:2511.12884
sources.quote: 2,303 agent context files from 1,925 repositories; 16 instruction types

sources.name: Jiang & Nam 2026 - Beyond the Prompt: Cursor Rules Empirical Study (arXiv:2512.18925, MSR'26)
sources.quote: Through a qualitative analysis of 401 open-source repositories containing cursor rules, we developed a comprehensive taxonomy of project context

etd_balance.benefices: Cohesion projet, reduction re-explication, onboarding agents
etd_balance.risques: Gloaguen 2026 paradoxe : context files peuvent REDUIRE task success et +20% cout
etd_balance.balance: Equilibre tendance Benefices si instructions minimales/non-inferables
